histories.bioA: Feuds openly with Dagny of the Wrackhouse, whose salvage he calls theft with paperwork.
histories.bioB: Keeps a running war with Brann the chandler, who sees his own labels under her sander.

histories.bioA: Holds paper on Tarry Jek's tide-finds - the mudlark's treasures reach the pawn window before they reach the light.
histories.bioB: Owes Fenner against every find the strand yields; the hook is old and deep.

histories.bioA: Quietly owns the Slop-Chest's stock past the third hook - Neddry signed in a bad winter.
histories.bioB: Signed Fenner's paper one bad winter; the slops on his own shelves are half the pawnbroker's now.

histories.bioA: Keeps one paper he never displays: the Widow Netter's, signed against the house itself.
histories.bioB: Signed quiet paper at Fenner's window after the boats were lost; the house stands on his patience.

histories.bioA: Nurses a sergeant's grudge against Master Venn - the Gull's back room has cost the Watch too many clean cases.
histories.bioB: Keeps Sergeant Vess's grudge on the ledger beside the breakage: the back room's games are worth both.

histories.bioA: Balances one Weighhouse number that never happened; Crell's erased line ends in his blotter.
histories.bioB: Shares with Master Gilt the one erased line in an otherwise honest ledger.

histories.bioA: Is paid handsomely by House Quayward to whistle while certain night cargo crosses his yard.
histories.bioB: Pays Harl's yard a whistling-wage for lighters that land after the Weighhouse sleeps.

histories.bioA: Feuds with Goodman Saltgate over the party wall - two spans of masonry, twenty years of lawyers.
histories.bioB: Contests two spans of party wall with the Netters' house; stubbornness is the family mortar.

histories.bioA: Counts Gullet Mag his one true friend in the ward - she asks no questions he would have to answer with lies.
histories.bioB: Keeps a soft spot for the quiet tenant Finch, and keeps whatever she has guessed about him grayer than her voice.

histories.bioA: Circles Sergeant Brakk for the captaincy both want; Saltgate reports against guardhouse cells.
histories.bioB: Fills K34's cells partly to spite Sergeant Vess's tidy Saltgate reports; one captaincy is coming.

histories.bioA: Has not forgiven Watchman Cull a mis-kenneled pup twenty years gone; the feud outlived the dog.
histories.bioB: Feuds with Old Cobb over a pup mis-kenneled twenty years back; neither man will bury it.

histories.bioA: Was promised to Maell once, before the Flame claimed them both for different vigils.
histories.bioB: Set aside an old promise to Sethra of the Lantern Room when the Flame called; the grief is shared and unspoken.

histories.bioA: Thickens the soup on the days Tarry Jek leaves tide-glass at the Mission step.
histories.bioB: Leaves his best tide-glass at the Mission step for Onna, and eats better for it than he admits.

histories.bioA: Orders more barrels than the catch requires; the cooper delivers each one himself.
histories.bioB: Delivers his best oak to Salla of Salt Row personally, and takes payment mostly in the walk back.

histories.bioA: Cuts canvas late whenever the Bilge's lamp burns; a tankard waits on his bench.
histories.bioB: Keeps a sailmaker's needle behind the bar and the Loft's lamp in the corner of her eye.

notes: Sprint-2 WORLD micro-histories (S2-1 'the stories between them'): authored feuds/debts/romances/pacts among the Forty Notables, realized at bake as real RelationshipRegistry edges plus one bio-addendum sentence per side (appended by NameForge after the notable's authored bio). Parties are notables.json ids - spawn-site-bound, never raw ActorIds. Edge vocabulary is the CURRENT append-only RelationshipKind enum (sim-owned): feud -> NEIGHBOR (a known-adversary adjacency tie; hostility carried by bios+gossip), debt -> MENTOR directed a(creditor) -> b(debtor) (the 'oversees' sense; no tick-path consumer), romance/pact/secret -> FRIEND (symmetric; BarkSelector greets these pairs as friends and Barter grants the kitchen-price Royal off, deliberately). RIVAL/DEBTOR/ROMANCE proper kinds are flagged to SIM-CORE for a future append; this file then migrates edge values without reshaping. Each history's gossip key must exist in barks/barks.json (asserted by DocksMicroHistoryTest).
